skill.name: google-maps-search-api-skill
skill.description: Automates extraction of business data from Google Maps search results including names, addresses, ratings, reviews, and contact details.
skill.summary: Automated Google Maps business data extraction via the BrowserAct API for lead generation and market research.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: All network findings are false positives - HTTP requests target the legitimate BrowserAct API (api.browseract.com) with hardcoded URLs, presenting no SSRF risk. The env_access finding is a documented and expected pattern: the skill reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables for standard API authentication, which is best practice. All 40 external_commands findings are false positives - backtick characters in SKILL.md are markdown formatting, not shell execution. The critical heuristic for dangerous combination is dismissed: no obfuscation or malicious code execution exists. Safe to publish with documented API key requirement.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Key
low_findings.description: The skill reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables using os.getenv(). This is standard and secure practice for API credential management. The key is only transmitted to the legitimate BrowserAct API endpoint at api.browseract.com over HTTPS. Confidence: 0.90 - confirmed legitimate access pattern for required API authentication.

low_findings.title: Network Requests to External API
low_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to api.browseract.com, an external third-party API. The API base URL is hardcoded and not user-configurable, eliminating SSRF risk. These requests are the core functionality of the skill. Confidence: 0.95 - hardcoded URL to a known legitimate service.

content.user_title: Extract Business Data from Google Maps
content.value_statement: Manually collecting business data from Google Maps is slow and error-prone. This skill automates extraction of business names, addresses, ratings, and contact details through the BrowserAct API for fast, structured results.
actual_capabilities: Search Google Maps by keyword and extract structured business data
Filter results by language and country for localized searches
Retrieve business names, addresses, ratings, and review counts
Extract price range, cuisine type, and amenity tags from listings
Get review snippets and service option details for each business
Configure maximum result count up to 100 entries per search
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key to function
Maximum of 100 results can be returned per search query
Data quality depends on Google Maps listing completeness
Task execution can take several minutes for large result sets

use_cases.title: Sales Lead Generation
use_cases.description: Extract lists of businesses in a target area for sales prospecting and outreach campaigns.
use_cases.target_user: Sales professionals and business development teams

use_cases.title: Market Research and Competitor Analysis
use_cases.description: Analyze competitor density, pricing ranges, ratings, and service offerings across geographic regions.

use_cases.title: Local Service Discovery
use_cases.description: Find specific services such as dental clinics, restaurants, or coworking spaces in unfamiliar areas.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Keyword Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Google Maps for coffee shops in San Francisco. Return the business names and addresses.
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple keyword-based search with no language or country filters

prompt_templates.title: Language-Specific Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Google Maps for Italian restaurants in Berlin with language set to de. Return names, ratings, and price ranges.
prompt_templates.scenario: Search with language filter for localized results

prompt_templates.title: Country-Targeted Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Google Maps for boutique hotels in Paris with country set to fr. Extract names, addresses, ratings, and review counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Country-specific search for regional business data

prompt_templates.title: Full Parameter Configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Google Maps for real estate agencies in London with language en and country gb. Set max results to 50. Return names, full addresses, ratings, review counts, and price ranges in a structured format.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced search with all parameters configured for maximum data completeness

output_examples.input: Find Italian restaurants in Manhattan with ratings and price ranges
output: Da Nico Restaurant - 164 Mulberry St - Rating: 4.5 - Reviews: 892 - Price: $$
Il Mulino New York - 86 W 3rd St - Rating: 4.6 - Reviews: 1204 - Price: $$$
Carmine's Italian Restaurant - 200 W 44th St - Rating: 4.3 - Reviews: 5678 - Price: $$

output_examples.input: Search for dental clinics in Sydney Australia with contact information
output: Sydney CBD Dental - 123 George St - Rating: 4.7 - Reviews: 234 - Price: $$ - Services: General dentistry, cosmetic dentistry
The Dental Practice - 45 King St - Rating: 4.5 - Reviews: 189 - Price: $$ - Services: Emergency dental, teeth whitening
Bondi Dental - 78 Hall St - Rating: 4.8 - Reviews: 456 - Price: $$$ - Services: Implants, orthodontics

output_examples.input: Search for coworking spaces in Berlin with amenities
output: Betahaus Berlin - Prinzessinnenstr. 19-20 - Rating: 4.3 - Amenities: Wi-Fi, printing, meeting rooms, café
Factory Berlin - Rheinsberger Str. 76/77 - Rating: 4.5 - Amenities: Wi-Fi, event space, rooftop terrace, kitchen
Ahoy Berlin - Oranienstr. 183 - Rating: 4.4 - Amenities: Wi-Fi, 3D printer, podcast studio, bike parking

best_practices: Set specific language and country parameters to get the most relevant results for your target region
Start with broad keywords and refine based on results to ensure full coverage of your search area
Verify extracted data against business websites before importing into CRM or production systems
anti_patterns: Using overly generic one-word search terms that return thousands of irrelevant or unfocused results
Making repeated identical searches at high frequency which may trigger API rate limits
Assuming all data fields such as price range or cuisine type are populated for every business listing

faq.question: What data fields can this skill extract from Google Maps?
faq.answer: It extracts business name, full address, star rating, review count, price range, cuisine type, amenity tags, review snippets, and service options.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a valid BrowserAct API key. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before using the skill.

faq.question: How long does a typical search take?
faq.answer: Search tasks can take several minutes depending on the number of results. The script outputs status logs with timestamps so you can monitor progress.

faq.question: What happens if my API key is invalid?
faq.answer: The script will output an Invalid authorization error message. You need to verify your API key in the BrowserAct console and provide a valid key.

faq.question: Can I search for businesses in any country?
faq.answer: Yes, you can set the country parameter to any two-letter country code such as us, gb, de, fr, jp, au, or ca to bias results to that region.

faq.question: Is there a limit on how many results I can get?
faq.answer: The maximum number of results per search is 100. You can configure this limit using the max_dates parameter.
